matches.x: CHAPTER VIL : Solomon persuadeth to sincere wisdom— The cunning of a whore. : Y son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.

with a double meaning: one the literal, the other figurative, as in the words “say to Wisdom, thou art my sister” (Prov. 7, 4). SAY NOW THOU ART MY SISTER.

AND THE MEN OF THE PLACE ASKED HIM OF HIS WIFE; AND HE SAID: SHE IS MY SISTER. Like Abraham before him, he referred with these words to the Shekinah, which was with him as well as with Rebekah his wife; for like Abraham he carried out the injunction: “Say unto wisdom: Thou art my sister” (Prov. 7, 4). They were further entitled to call her sister in virtue of the verse, “My sister, my love, my…
Abraham 2 25 Therefore say unto them, I pray thee, thou art my sister, that it may be well with me for thy sake, and my soul shall live because of thee.
Say, I pray, that thou art my sister, that it may be well with me for thy sake, and that my life may be spared on thy account.

Window of my house Solomon, still speaking to his son hoping for him to identify with the "young man void of understanding." The "casement" was a lattice perhaps in the role of the old shutters.

I called on Sister Robinson in the twilight of evening, and
asked for the dinner which I had spoken for one and a half years
before. We were kindly received by her and others.
Evening, in the black and dark of night Literally, "in the pupil [of the eye] of the night."

Then followed the special sacrificial offerings for the day and the chanting, to instrumental

The executioner must have gone a long journey, and which could scarcely be performed in two days from Tiberias, where the tyrant's court was, to execute that bloody command. So that that horrid dish, the head of the venerable prophet, could not be presented to the maid but some days after the celebration of his birthday.
